23	## The problem / 问题
24	
25	> 简述：现在举措一旦发布，它的完成率就一定会按权重计入周期最终分，没有任何办法把某一条举措排除掉。结转过来的举措（上周期没做完、这周期接着做）就成了麻烦：要么留着它、让没做完的旧工作把这周期的分拉低；要么把它删掉、就再也跟不上它的进度了。客户要的是"既能继续跟踪、又不计分"，现在系统给不了。
26	
27	Today, once an initiative is published, its completion rate is always weighted into the strategy-cycle final score. There is no way to keep an initiative in the system without it counting.
28	
29	This is a real problem for **carry-over initiatives** — work that started in a prior cycle, was not finished, and continues into the next cycle:
30	
31	- **Keep it as-is** → the unfinished prior-cycle work drags down the new cycle's score, even though that work does not belong to the new cycle's performance.
32	- **Delete it** → the team loses the ability to track and report on that ongoing work.
33	
34	The customer needs a third option: keep the initiative visible and trackable, but exclude it from the score. The system cannot do this today.
35	
36	| 现状 / Current | 缺口 / Gap |
37	|---|---|
38	| 已发布的举措必然按权重计入周期最终分 / Published initiatives are always weighted into the cycle score | 没有"保留可见、但不计分"的中间状态 / No "visible but not counted" middle state |
39	| KPI 已经有"计入 / 不计入统计"开关 / KPIs already have a Counted / Excluded switch | 举措没有对应的开关 / Initiatives have no equivalent switch |
40	
41	---
42	
43	## Current behavior / 当前行为
44	
45	> 简述：举措创建/编辑表单里没有"是否计入统计"这个字段。一条发布的举措 100% 会被算进它所属周期的最终分。结转的举措只能照样计分，或者干脆删掉。
46	
47	- The initiative create / edit form has **no field controlling whether the initiative counts toward statistics**. Every published initiative is implicitly counted.
48	- When the strategy-cycle final score is computed, every published initiative in the cycle contributes its weighted completion rate. There is no exclusion path.
49	- A carry-over initiative therefore either counts in full (diluting the new cycle's score) or has to be removed entirely.
50	
51	By contrast, a KPI already offers a **Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats** choice on its form; an excluded KPI stays visible and in the list but is left out of the score. Initiatives have no parallel.
52	
53	---
54	
55	## Expected behavior / 期望行为
56	
57	> 简述：举措的创建/编辑表单加一个"是否计入统计"选择（默认"计入统计"），跟 KPI 那个一模一样。设成"不计入统计"后：这条举措在举措列表、详情、绩效报表里照常显示，照常能更新进度，但算周期最终分的时候被排除 —— 既不进分子也不进分母，不会稀释这周期实际做的工作的得分。列表里给"不计入统计"的举措一个标记，让人一眼看出它不参与计分。结转一条没做完的举措时，用户在新周期里把它的开关设成"不计入统计"就行，不需要复制出一条新举措。
58	
59	The initiative create / edit form gains a single new **mandatory** field — labeled **"Type"** (the same label the KPI form uses for its Counted/Excluded setting, for consistency) — with two options **Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats**, defaulting to **Counted in Stats**. It sits in the **General Info section, directly under the date** field. It mirrors the KPI setting one-to-one.
60	
61	| Field property / 字段属性 | Value / 值 |
62	|---|---|
63	| Label / 标签 | **Type** (same as the KPI form's Counted/Excluded field) / 跟 KPI 表单一致，叫 "Type" |
64	| Section / 位置 | General Info, under the date / 基本信息区，日期下面 |
65	| Options / 选项 | Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats |
66	| Required? / 是否必填 | **Mandatory** / 必填 |
67	| Default / 默认 | Counted in Stats / 计入统计 |
68	
69	When an initiative is set to **Excluded from Stats**:
70	
71	- It still appears in the **initiatives list**, its **detail page**, and the **performance report / dashboard** — nothing disappears.
72	- It is still fully **editable** — owners update its completion rate as normal.
73	- It is **left out of the strategy-cycle final-score calculation** — it neither contributes its weighted completion rate nor inflates the weight base, so it does not dilute the score earned by work actually belonging to the cycle.
74	- It carries a clear **marker** in the list so a reader can see at a glance that it does not count.
75	
76	The field is **general-purpose**. The user sets it on whichever initiative they don't want counted — the system does no carry-over detection. For the carry-over use case, the user creates the initiative they want to continue tracking (as they do today) and sets its choice to **Excluded from Stats**; it then stays trackable and reportable but stops contributing to the cycle score. The same field can be used for any other initiative the user wants kept out of the score.
77	
78	**All existing / historical initiatives default to Counted in Stats** — so nothing about current data's scoring changes; exclusion is only ever an explicit choice going forward.
79	
80	| 设置 / Setting | 列表与详情可见 / Visible in list & detail | 可更新进度 / Updatable | 计入周期最终分 / Counts toward cycle score |
81	|---|---|---|---|
82	| Counted in Stats（默认 / default） | 是 / Yes | 是 / Yes | 是 / Yes |
83	| Excluded from Stats | 是 / Yes | 是 / Yes | 否 / No |
84	
85	| Expected — "Type" field in General Info, under the date / 期望 — 基本信息区日期下面的 "Type" 字段 |
86	|---|
87	| ![expected](artifacts/screenshots/initiative-exclude-stats/expected-type-field.png) |
88	
89	> The required "Type" field (default Counted in Stats) sits in General Info directly under the Start/End date, mirroring the KPI form's Counted/Excluded field. (Local prototype, 2026-06-15.)
90	
91	### Where exclusion applies — and where it must NOT / 排除在哪些地方生效、哪些地方不能生效
92	
93	> 简述：经过排查 —— "不计入统计"只应该影响"绩效得分"类计算，不应该影响"可见性""提醒""可下钻列表"。排除的举措在列表、详情、Ongoing Cycle、迟交、提醒邮件里照常出现/照常发，只是从各处的"绩效分"里去掉。有一个产品决策点：预算/数量统计要不要也排除（见待澄清）。系统排查确认：没有任何地方会因为排除而报错，全部被排除的目标（分母为 0）也安全（各处都有零值保护）。
94	
95	A code review of every place that consumes an initiative's performance confirms exclusion is **safe** — nothing breaks — but it must be applied **only to score/performance calculations**, never to visibility or reminders:
96	
97	| Consumer / 消费方 | Excluded initiative behavior / 排除后的行为 |
98	|---|---|
99	| Objective / cycle / pillar performance rollup | **Filtered out of the score** (numerator + weight base) — clean, denominators are zero-guarded |
100	| Objective detail statistics | Filtered out of the score — clean |
101	| Dashboard performance rings + Sector/Monthly/Performance reports | **Filtered out of the score** — note these average over the item set, so the filter must be applied at the source query (so an excluded item doesn't deflate the average) |
102	| Initiatives **list**, **detail page** | **Still shown** — never filtered |
103	| **Budget / expense totals + initiative counts** (dashboard) | **Still included** — an excluded initiative still exists and still has real budget; exclusion is **score-only** (see Locked decision) |
104	| Ongoing Cycle list, Late-Submission, reminder emails | **Still shown / still sent** — excluded initiatives are still real work in progress; do NOT suppress these |
105	| Activities, linked KPIs, approval workflow | Unaffected — no dependency on the stat flag |
106	
107	**No place breaks.** The "all initiatives under an objective excluded" edge case (denominator 0) is safe everywhere — every divisor is either a constant or wrapped in a zero/empty guard.
108	
109	---
110	
111	## Acceptance criteria / 验收标准
112	
113	| # | Criterion | Priority |
114	|---|---|---|
115	| 1 | The initiative create / edit form shows a **mandatory "Type" field** (same label as the KPI form's Counted/Excluded field) in the **General Info section, under the date**, with two options matching the KPI wording exactly (Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats) | P0 |
116	| 2 | The field is **required** and **defaults to Counted in Stats**; the form is valid with the default without the user touching it, but the field cannot be cleared to empty | P0 |
117	| 3 | The choice is editable on an existing initiative — any initiative can be switched between Counted and Excluded at any time | P0 |
118	| 3b | All existing / historical initiatives default to Counted in Stats after the migration — no current scoring changes | P0 |
119	| 4 | An initiative set to Excluded from Stats still appears in the initiatives list, its detail page, and the performance report / dashboard, and remains fully editable | P0 |
120	| 5 | An initiative set to Excluded from Stats is left out of the strategy-cycle final-score calculation — it contributes neither its weighted completion rate nor its weight to the score base | P0 |
121	| 6 | An initiative set to Counted in Stats behaves exactly as today — counted in the score | P0 |
122	| 7 | The initiatives list shows a visible marker on Excluded-from-Stats initiatives so a reader can tell at a glance that they do not count | P1 |
123	| 8 | The setting persists per initiative and survives reload; switching it and re-opening the form shows the saved value | P0 |
124	| 9 | The new field renders correctly in both English and Arabic | P1 |
125	
126	---
127	
128	## QA test scenario / QA 测试场景
129	
130	| Setup | Steps | Expected |
131	|---|---|---|
132	| Admin role. A strategy cycle with at least two published initiatives; the cycle's final score is visible on the performance report. One initiative represents carry-over work that should not count. | 1. Open the initiatives list and note the cycle's current final score on the performance report. 2. Open the carry-over initiative for edit — confirm the new "counted in statistics" choice is present and currently set to the default Counted in Stats. 3. Change it to Excluded from Stats and save. 4. Reopen the initiative — confirm the saved value is Excluded from Stats. 5. Return to the initiatives list — confirm the initiative is still listed with a marker indicating it does not count, and is still editable. 6. Update the excluded initiative's completion rate and save. 7. Recheck the performance report / dashboard — confirm the excluded initiative is still shown but the cycle final score is now computed without it. 8. Switch the other initiative to Excluded as well, then back to Counted, and confirm the score recomputes correctly each time. 9. Switch the UI to Arabic and reopen the form — confirm the field and its options render in Arabic. | Step 2: field present, defaulting to Counted in Stats. Step 4: Excluded value persisted across reload. Step 5: initiative remains visible + editable with a "not counted" marker. Step 7: excluded initiative still appears in the report but no longer affects the final score; the score reflects only counted initiatives. Step 8: score recomputes correctly on each toggle. Step 9: Arabic labels render. |
133	
134	---
135	
136	## Proposed technical solution
137	
138	> English-only section. PM does not need to review beyond this point.
139	
140	This is a direct port of the existing KPI "exclude from stats" mechanism to the Initiative entity. The KPI implementation is the proven precedent and is reused for wording, enum semantics, and the aggregation-filter approach.
141	
142	### KPI precedent (what we are mirroring)
143	
144	- Enum: `KpiStatTypeEnum` — `COUNTED(0)` / `EXCLUDED(1)`, at `backend/service/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/biz/service/kpis/enums/KpiStatTypeEnum.java`. Frontend mirror: `strategyApiTypeEnum` (`Counted in Stats = 0` / `Excluded from Stats = 1`) at `frontend/src/constants/strategicKpis.ts:56-59`.
145	- KPI column: `KpisEntity.type` (`@TableField("type")`, "0=计入统计，1=不计入") at `backend/dao/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/dao/entity/kpis/KpisEntity.java:92-94`.
146	- KPI aggregation filter — excluded KPIs are filtered out of the score, not deleted:
147	  - `CycleInfoServiceImpl.java:1701` — `.eq(KpisEntity::getType, KpiStatTypeEnum.COUNTED.getCode())`
148	  - `ReportServiceImpl.java:439` and `:968` — `.eq(KpisEntity::getType, 0)`
149	- KPI form field — `field={'type'}` with `strategyApiTypeOptions`, at `frontend/src/pages/StrategicKpis/KpisManagement/AddKpi/components/KpiForm.tsx:1499-1525`; options built at lines 445-454; locale keys `kpis:add.type.inStats` / `kpis:add.type.exStats` and `kpis:informationTypes.inStats` / `exStats` in `frontend/src/locale/en/kpis.json` (Arabic in `arEG/kpis.json`).
150	
151	### Backend
152	
153	1. **New column on `sk_initiative`.** Add a stat-type flag to `InitiativeEntity` (`backend/dao/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/dao/entity/initiative/InitiativeEntity.java`; currently has no such flag). Suggested: `private Integer statType;` mapped to a new column (e.g. `stat_type`), `0 = Counted`, `1 = Excluded`, **default 0**. Add the column to `backend/dao/src/main/resources/mapper/initiative/InitiativeMapper.xml` (column block at lines 7-33). Migration backfills existing rows to `0` (counted) to preserve current behavior. Reuse `KpiStatTypeEnum` or introduce an `InitiativeStatTypeEnum` with identical `COUNTED(0)/EXCLUDED(1)` semantics.
154	
155	2. **Filter the cycle-score aggregation.** The initiative aggregation lives in `CycleInfoServiceImpl.getObjectivePerformance()` at `backend/service/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/biz/service/cycle/impl/CycleInfoServiceImpl.java:1692-1760`. The initiative query is at line 1693 (currently filters only `status = PUBLISHED`); the weighted sum is at lines 1736-1744 and the cycle-level normalization at lines 1757-1759. Add `.eq(InitiativeEntity::getStatType, InitiativeStatTypeEnum.COUNTED.getCode())` to the query at line 1693 so excluded initiatives drop out of **both** the weighted numerator and the `totalWeight` denominator — matching how the KPI exclusion behaves (no dilution).
156	
157	3. **Keep list / report queries unfiltered.** The dashboard/report initiative query `statisticsInitiativePerformance()` in `backend/dao/src/main/resources/mapper/initiative/InitiativeMapper.xml:54-92` (filters `status = 3`, `is_deleted = 0`) and the standard list/detail queries must **not** add the stat-type filter — excluded initiatives stay visible. Confirm the report's score figure derives from the cycle aggregation (step 2), not from a separate sum of this list, so exclusion is honored where it matters and visibility is preserved elsewhere. (Open question: whether the report's headline score reads from `getObjectivePerformance` or recomputes from `statisticsInitiativePerformance` — verify during impl.)
158	
159	4. **Persist the field.** Add `statType` to the save/edit request DTO `InitiativeSaveRequest` (`backend/web/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/web/controller/initiative/request/InitiativeSaveRequest.java`, current fields lines 24-103) and map it through `InitiativeConverter.convertInitiativeSave`. Endpoints: `POST /api/initiative/save` and `POST /api/initiative/editInfo` (`backend/web/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/web/controller/initiative/InitiativeController.java:113-129`). Default to `0` when the client omits it.
160	
161	### Frontend
162	
163	1. **Form field.** Add a **required `Type` `Select`** to `frontend/src/pages/Initiatives/InitiativesMgmt/components/InitiativesAddAndEdit/InitiativesAddForm.tsx`, placed in the **General Info section directly under the date** field (not next to Budget). Label it **"Type"** to match the KPI form's Counted/Excluded field label. Mirror the KPI field: `field={'statType'}`, `rules={[{ required: true }]}`, `initialValue={strategyApiTypeEnum['Counted in Stats']}`, options reusing `strategyApiTypeEnum` and the `kpis:informationTypes.inStats` / `exStats` labels (or new initiative-namespaced keys with identical text).
164	
165	2. **State / type.** Add `statType?: number` to `initiativeGeneralInfoState` in `frontend/src/store/initiative.ts:11-38`, and thread it through the save/edit payloads `initiativeInfoSave` / `initiativeInfoEdit` in `frontend/src/service/apis/initiative.ts`.
166	
167	3. **List marker.** In the initiatives list, render a neutral/grey Arco `Tag` (no icon/emoji) on rows where `statType === 1`, with a tooltip explaining "Excluded from the cycle score; progress is still tracked." Mirror the tag conventions used elsewhere in the system.
168	
169	4. **Locale.** Add EN + AR keys under the initiatives namespace (`frontend/src/locale/en/initiatives.json`, `frontend/src/locale/arEG/initiatives.json`); the option text can reuse the KPI strings to stay identical.
170	
171	### Effort estimate
172	
173	- Backend: ~0.5 day (column + migration + one aggregation filter + DTO field).
174	- Frontend: ~0.5 day (form field + list marker + locale + type plumbing).
175	- QA: ~0.5 day.
176	- **Total: ~1.5 dev-days.**
177	
178	### Feasibility verdict (for the dev team)
179	
180	Workable and low-risk. The initiative aggregation query (`CycleInfoServiceImpl:1693`) is **separate** from the list/detail/report queries, so a single flag cleanly removes an initiative from the score while leaving it visible everywhere else — exactly how the KPI flag already behaves. **A duplicate initiative is not required**; a single `statType` flag on the existing initiative, toggled when it carries over, fully satisfies "visible + reportable but not counted."
181	
182	---
183	
184	## Open questions / 待澄清
185	
186	> Minor items only — the score-only scope is now locked (see below).
187	
188	1. **List marker wording & style.** What label/tooltip text should the list marker use ("Not counted" / "Excluded from stats"), and should the report add a footnote noting excluded initiatives? Proposed default: a neutral grey tag "Excluded from stats" with a tooltip "Tracked, but not counted in the cycle score." / 列表标记文案与样式；建议用中性灰标签 + 提示。
189	
190	2. **Who can set it / role visibility.** Available to all initiative editors, or admin-only? Proposed default: same edit permission as the rest of the initiative form (whoever can edit the initiative can set this field). / 谁能设 —— 建议跟编辑举措的权限一致。
191	
192	3. **⚠ Budget rollup for excluded initiatives — PENDING CUSTOMER CONFIRMATION (Amir 2026-06-17).** Locked decision 8 currently keeps an excluded initiative's budget/expense **in** the dashboard totals (score-only exclusion). Amir flagged uncertainty: the dashboard budget *might* be using **last year's budget figure**, so confirm with the customer (a) whether an excluded initiative's budget should still be counted, and (b) which budget figure the dashboard actually sums (current vs prior cycle). Until confirmed, decision 8 (budget still counted) stands as the default. / 预算合计是否计入被排除举措、且仪表盘用的是当年还是去年预算，待客户确认；在确认前按决策 8（仍计入）执行。
193	
194	---
195	
196	## Locked decisions / 已锁定决策
197	
198	> Only decisions directly implied by the confirmed requirement.
199	
200	1. **A plain general-purpose field — single flag, NOT a carry-over toggle.** R-450 adds one `statType` field to the initiative (mirroring KPI). It is NOT tied to carry-over logic; the system does no carry-over detection and nothing automatic. A duplicate initiative is not required — the user just sets the flag on whichever initiative they want excluded. Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 就是一个普通字段（单开关），不绑定结转逻辑，系统不做任何自动判断；不需要复制举措。
201	
202	2. **No carry-over auto-detection.** The old draft's "detect incomplete prior-cycle initiatives and prompt the admin" banner is OUT of scope. Users manually create and flag initiatives themselves. Reason: a system that has to interpret "is this a carry-over?" is fragile; a plain field the user controls is simpler and more flexible. Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 不做"自动检测上周期未完成举措并提示"那套，纯手动。
203	
204	3. **All existing / historical initiatives default to Counted in Stats.** The migration backfills every current initiative to Counted, so no current data's scoring changes. Exclusion is only ever an explicit forward choice. Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 所有历史举措默认"计入统计"，不改变现状。
205	
206	4. **Mechanism mirrors the KPI exclude-from-stats setting.** Wording and semantics follow the KPI "Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats" precedent. / 机制照搬 KPI 的"计入/不计入统计"，文案语义一致。
207	
208	4b. **Field label = "Type"**, the same label the KPI form uses for its Counted/Excluded field (consistency). Placed in the **General Info section, under the date**. The field is **mandatory** (defaults to Counted, cannot be cleared to empty). Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 字段叫 "Type"（跟 KPI 一致），放在基本信息区日期下面，必填，默认计入。
209	
210	5. **Default is Counted in Stats.** A newly-created initiative counts toward the score by default; exclusion is an explicit opt-in. / 默认"计入统计"。
211	
212	6. **Excluded initiatives stay visible, updatable, and reportable.** Exclusion affects only the final-score calculation; the initiative remains in lists, detail, and reports. / 排除只影响最终计分，举措照常显示、可更新。
213	
214	7. **Excluded from denominator, not zeroed.** An excluded initiative is removed from both the weighted numerator and the weight base, so it does not dilute the score — matching the KPI exclusion behavior. / 不计入时从分子和分母一起去掉，不是清零。
215	
216	8. **Exclusion is SCORE-ONLY.** "Excluded from Stats" removes the initiative only from performance / score calculations (objective & cycle rollup, dashboard performance rings, performance/monthly/sector reports). It is **still counted** in budget/expense totals and initiative counts, and stays visible in lists, detail, Ongoing Cycle, late-submission, and still receives reminder emails — because it is still a real initiative with real budget and work in progress. Matches the KPI precedent. Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 排除只影响绩效得分；预算合计、举措数量照常计入，列表/详情/进行中/迟交/提醒邮件照常。
217	
218	---
219	
220	## Related PRDs / 相关 PRD
221	
222	- [R-576 KPI List — Completion Rate Column Fix for Excluded Percentage KPIs](../V3.3/[R-576]-excluded-percentage-completion-rate-display.md) — the KPI exclude-from-stats precedent this PRD ports to initiatives (`KpiStatTypeEnum` / `strategyApiTypeEnum`).
223	- [R-579 Committee Add KPI Form Fields](./[R-579]-committee-add-kpi-form-fields.md) — same-version KPI form work using the Counted/Excluded stat-type field; the initiative field reuses its option wording.
224

file.content:
# [P1] R-450 — "Exclude from Statistics" Field for Initiatives / 举措的"不计入统计"字段

| | |
|---|---|
| **Version** | V3.4 |
| **Source / 来源** | Existing draft ticket refined at the 2026-06-10 V3.4 planning meeting (Initiatives — Carry-over Score Contribution) / 既有草稿需求，在 2026-06-10 V3.4 规划会议上细化（举措结转计分） |
| **Priority** | P1 |
| **Created** | 2026-06-15 |
| **PM status** | `draft` |

---

## Overview / 概述

> 简述：给举措加一个普通的"是否计入统计"字段，跟 KPI 已有的"计入统计 / 不计入统计"完全一样。设成"不计入统计"后，这条举措在列表、详情、报表里照常显示、照常能更新，只是算周期最终分时被排除。这是一个**通用字段**，不绑定任何"结转"逻辑 —— 系统不去判断哪条是结转举措、也不自动处理。用户什么时候想让某条举措不计分，自己手动把它设成"不计入"就行（典型场景：把上周期没做完的工作在新周期里建一条新举措、设成不计入；但也可以用于任何其他不想计分的情况）。

R-450 adds a plain **"Exclude from Statistics" field** to the initiative — working exactly like the existing **Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats** setting on KPIs. When an initiative is set to Excluded, it still appears in lists, detail pages, and reports and stays fully updatable, but it is left out of the final cycle-score calculation.

This is a **general-purpose field, not tied to any carry-over logic.** The system does **not** detect "carry-over" initiatives or do anything automatically — it simply honors the flag the user sets. The user manually decides which initiative should not count and sets it themselves. The motivating use case is carry-over work (an initiative that didn't finish in a prior cycle, recreated in the new cycle and marked Excluded so it's tracked but doesn't dilute the score), but the field works for any "track it but don't count it" need.

---

## The problem / 问题

> 简述：现在举措一旦发布，它的完成率就一定会按权重计入周期最终分，没有任何办法把某一条举措排除掉。结转过来的举措（上周期没做完、这周期接着做）就成了麻烦：要么留着它、让没做完的旧工作把这周期的分拉低；要么把它删掉、就再也跟不上它的进度了。客户要的是"既能继续跟踪、又不计分"，现在系统给不了。

Today, once an initiative is published, its completion rate is always weighted into the strategy-cycle final score. There is no way to keep an initiative in the system without it counting.

This is a real problem for **carry-over initiatives** — work that started in a prior cycle, was not finished, and continues into the next cycle:

- **Keep it as-is** → the unfinished prior-cycle work drags down the new cycle's score, even though that work does not belong to the new cycle's performance.
- **Delete it** → the team loses the ability to track and report on that ongoing work.

The customer needs a third option: keep the initiative visible and trackable, but exclude it from the score. The system cannot do this today.

| 现状 / Current | 缺口 / Gap |
|---|---|
| 已发布的举措必然按权重计入周期最终分 / Published initiatives are always weighted into the cycle score | 没有"保留可见、但不计分"的中间状态 / No "visible but not counted" middle state |
| KPI 已经有"计入 / 不计入统计"开关 / KPIs already have a Counted / Excluded switch | 举措没有对应的开关 / Initiatives have no equivalent switch |

---

## Current behavior / 当前行为

> 简述：举措创建/编辑表单里没有"是否计入统计"这个字段。一条发布的举措 100% 会被算进它所属周期的最终分。结转的举措只能照样计分，或者干脆删掉。

- The initiative create / edit form has **no field controlling whether the initiative counts toward statistics**. Every published initiative is implicitly counted.
- When the strategy-cycle final score is computed, every published initiative in the cycle contributes its weighted completion rate. There is no exclusion path.
- A carry-over initiative therefore either counts in full (diluting the new cycle's score) or has to be removed entirely.

By contrast, a KPI already offers a **Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats** choice on its form; an excluded KPI stays visible and in the list but is left out of the score. Initiatives have no parallel.

---

## Expected behavior / 期望行为

> 简述：举措的创建/编辑表单加一个"是否计入统计"选择（默认"计入统计"），跟 KPI 那个一模一样。设成"不计入统计"后：这条举措在举措列表、详情、绩效报表里照常显示，照常能更新进度，但算周期最终分的时候被排除 —— 既不进分子也不进分母，不会稀释这周期实际做的工作的得分。列表里给"不计入统计"的举措一个标记，让人一眼看出它不参与计分。结转一条没做完的举措时，用户在新周期里把它的开关设成"不计入统计"就行，不需要复制出一条新举措。

The initiative create / edit form gains a single new **mandatory** field — labeled **"Type"** (the same label the KPI form uses for its Counted/Excluded setting, for consistency) — with two options **Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats**, defaulting to **Counted in Stats**. It sits in the **General Info section, directly under the date** field. It mirrors the KPI setting one-to-one.

| Field property / 字段属性 | Value / 值 |
|---|---|
| Label / 标签 | **Type** (same as the KPI form's Counted/Excluded field) / 跟 KPI 表单一致，叫 "Type" |
| Section / 位置 | General Info, under the date / 基本信息区，日期下面 |
| Options / 选项 | Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats |
| Required? / 是否必填 | **Mandatory** / 必填 |
| Default / 默认 | Counted in Stats / 计入统计 |

When an initiative is set to **Excluded from Stats**:

- It still appears in the **initiatives list**, its **detail page**, and the **performance report / dashboard** — nothing disappears.
- It is still fully **editable** — owners update its completion rate as normal.
- It is **left out of the strategy-cycle final-score calculation** — it neither contributes its weighted completion rate nor inflates the weight base, so it does not dilute the score earned by work actually belonging to the cycle.
- It carries a clear **marker** in the list so a reader can see at a glance that it does not count.

The field is **general-purpose**. The user sets it on whichever initiative they don't want counted — the system does no carry-over detection. For the carry-over use case, the user creates the initiative they want to continue tracking (as they do today) and sets its choice to **Excluded from Stats**; it then stays trackable and reportable but stops contributing to the cycle score. The same field can be used for any other initiative the user wants kept out of the score.

**All existing / historical initiatives default to Counted in Stats** — so nothing about current data's scoring changes; exclusion is only ever an explicit choice going forward.

| 设置 / Setting | 列表与详情可见 / Visible in list & detail | 可更新进度 / Updatable | 计入周期最终分 / Counts toward cycle score |
|---|---|---|---|
| Counted in Stats（默认 / default） | 是 / Yes | 是 / Yes | 是 / Yes |
| Excluded from Stats | 是 / Yes | 是 / Yes | 否 / No |

| Expected — "Type" field in General Info, under the date / 期望 — 基本信息区日期下面的 "Type" 字段 |
|---|
| ![expected](artifacts/screenshots/initiative-exclude-stats/expected-type-field.png) |

> The required "Type" field (default Counted in Stats) sits in General Info directly under the Start/End date, mirroring the KPI form's Counted/Excluded field. (Local prototype, 2026-06-15.)

### Where exclusion applies — and where it must NOT / 排除在哪些地方生效、哪些地方不能生效

> 简述：经过排查 —— "不计入统计"只应该影响"绩效得分"类计算，不应该影响"可见性""提醒""可下钻列表"。排除的举措在列表、详情、Ongoing Cycle、迟交、提醒邮件里照常出现/照常发，只是从各处的"绩效分"里去掉。有一个产品决策点：预算/数量统计要不要也排除（见待澄清）。系统排查确认：没有任何地方会因为排除而报错，全部被排除的目标（分母为 0）也安全（各处都有零值保护）。

A code review of every place that consumes an initiative's performance confirms exclusion is **safe** — nothing breaks — but it must be applied **only to score/performance calculations**, never to visibility or reminders:

| Consumer / 消费方 | Excluded initiative behavior / 排除后的行为 |
|---|---|
| Objective / cycle / pillar performance rollup | **Filtered out of the score** (numerator + weight base) — clean, denominators are zero-guarded |
| Objective detail statistics | Filtered out of the score — clean |
| Dashboard performance rings + Sector/Monthly/Performance reports | **Filtered out of the score** — note these average over the item set, so the filter must be applied at the source query (so an excluded item doesn't deflate the average) |
| Initiatives **list**, **detail page** | **Still shown** — never filtered |
| **Budget / expense totals + initiative counts** (dashboard) | **Still included** — an excluded initiative still exists and still has real budget; exclusion is **score-only** (see Locked decision) |
| Ongoing Cycle list, Late-Submission, reminder emails | **Still shown / still sent** — excluded initiatives are still real work in progress; do NOT suppress these |
| Activities, linked KPIs, approval workflow | Unaffected — no dependency on the stat flag |

**No place breaks.** The "all initiatives under an objective excluded" edge case (denominator 0) is safe everywhere — every divisor is either a constant or wrapped in a zero/empty guard.

---

## Acceptance criteria / 验收标准

| # | Criterion | Priority |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | The initiative create / edit form shows a **mandatory "Type" field** (same label as the KPI form's Counted/Excluded field) in the **General Info section, under the date**, with two options matching the KPI wording exactly (Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats) | P0 |
| 2 | The field is **required** and **defaults to Counted in Stats**; the form is valid with the default without the user touching it, but the field cannot be cleared to empty | P0 |
| 3 | The choice is editable on an existing initiative — any initiative can be switched between Counted and Excluded at any time | P0 |
| 3b | All existing / historical initiatives default to Counted in Stats after the migration — no current scoring changes | P0 |
| 4 | An initiative set to Excluded from Stats still appears in the initiatives list, its detail page, and the performance report / dashboard, and remains fully editable | P0 |
| 5 | An initiative set to Excluded from Stats is left out of the strategy-cycle final-score calculation — it contributes neither its weighted completion rate nor its weight to the score base | P0 |
| 6 | An initiative set to Counted in Stats behaves exactly as today — counted in the score | P0 |
| 7 | The initiatives list shows a visible marker on Excluded-from-Stats initiatives so a reader can tell at a glance that they do not count | P1 |
| 8 | The setting persists per initiative and survives reload; switching it and re-opening the form shows the saved value | P0 |
| 9 | The new field renders correctly in both English and Arabic | P1 |

---

## QA test scenario / QA 测试场景

| Setup | Steps | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| Admin role. A strategy cycle with at least two published initiatives; the cycle's final score is visible on the performance report. One initiative represents carry-over work that should not count. | 1. Open the initiatives list and note the cycle's current final score on the performance report. 2. Open the carry-over initiative for edit — confirm the new "counted in statistics" choice is present and currently set to the default Counted in Stats. 3. Change it to Excluded from Stats and save. 4. Reopen the initiative — confirm the saved value is Excluded from Stats. 5. Return to the initiatives list — confirm the initiative is still listed with a marker indicating it does not count, and is still editable. 6. Update the excluded initiative's completion rate and save. 7. Recheck the performance report / dashboard — confirm the excluded initiative is still shown but the cycle final score is now computed without it. 8. Switch the other initiative to Excluded as well, then back to Counted, and confirm the score recomputes correctly each time. 9. Switch the UI to Arabic and reopen the form — confirm the field and its options render in Arabic. | Step 2: field present, defaulting to Counted in Stats. Step 4: Excluded value persisted across reload. Step 5: initiative remains visible + editable with a "not counted" marker. Step 7: excluded initiative still appears in the report but no longer affects the final score; the score reflects only counted initiatives. Step 8: score recomputes correctly on each toggle. Step 9: Arabic labels render. |

---

## Proposed technical solution

> English-only section. PM does not need to review beyond this point.

This is a direct port of the existing KPI "exclude from stats" mechanism to the Initiative entity. The KPI implementation is the proven precedent and is reused for wording, enum semantics, and the aggregation-filter approach.

### KPI precedent (what we are mirroring)

- Enum: `KpiStatTypeEnum` — `COUNTED(0)` / `EXCLUDED(1)`, at `backend/service/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/biz/service/kpis/enums/KpiStatTypeEnum.java`. Frontend mirror: `strategyApiTypeEnum` (`Counted in Stats = 0` / `Excluded from Stats = 1`) at `frontend/src/constants/strategicKpis.ts:56-59`.
- KPI column: `KpisEntity.type` (`@TableField("type")`, "0=计入统计，1=不计入") at `backend/dao/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/dao/entity/kpis/KpisEntity.java:92-94`.
- KPI aggregation filter — excluded KPIs are filtered out of the score, not deleted:
  - `CycleInfoServiceImpl.java:1701` — `.eq(KpisEntity::getType, KpiStatTypeEnum.COUNTED.getCode())`
  - `ReportServiceImpl.java:439` and `:968` — `.eq(KpisEntity::getType, 0)`
- KPI form field — `field={'type'}` with `strategyApiTypeOptions`, at `frontend/src/pages/StrategicKpis/KpisManagement/AddKpi/components/KpiForm.tsx:1499-1525`; options built at lines 445-454; locale keys `kpis:add.type.inStats` / `kpis:add.type.exStats` and `kpis:informationTypes.inStats` / `exStats` in `frontend/src/locale/en/kpis.json` (Arabic in `arEG/kpis.json`).

### Backend

1. **New column on `sk_initiative`.** Add a stat-type flag to `InitiativeEntity` (`backend/dao/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/dao/entity/initiative/InitiativeEntity.java`; currently has no such flag). Suggested: `private Integer statType;` mapped to a new column (e.g. `stat_type`), `0 = Counted`, `1 = Excluded`, **default 0**. Add the column to `backend/dao/src/main/resources/mapper/initiative/InitiativeMapper.xml` (column block at lines 7-33). Migration backfills existing rows to `0` (counted) to preserve current behavior. Reuse `KpiStatTypeEnum` or introduce an `InitiativeStatTypeEnum` with identical `COUNTED(0)/EXCLUDED(1)` semantics.

2. **Filter the cycle-score aggregation.** The initiative aggregation lives in `CycleInfoServiceImpl.getObjectivePerformance()` at `backend/service/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/biz/service/cycle/impl/CycleInfoServiceImpl.java:1692-1760`. The initiative query is at line 1693 (currently filters only `status = PUBLISHED`); the weighted sum is at lines 1736-1744 and the cycle-level normalization at lines 1757-1759. Add `.eq(InitiativeEntity::getStatType, InitiativeStatTypeEnum.COUNTED.getCode())` to the query at line 1693 so excluded initiatives drop out of **both** the weighted numerator and the `totalWeight` denominator — matching how the KPI exclusion behaves (no dilution).

3. **Keep list / report queries unfiltered.** The dashboard/report initiative query `statisticsInitiativePerformance()` in `backend/dao/src/main/resources/mapper/initiative/InitiativeMapper.xml:54-92` (filters `status = 3`, `is_deleted = 0`) and the standard list/detail queries must **not** add the stat-type filter — excluded initiatives stay visible. Confirm the report's score figure derives from the cycle aggregation (step 2), not from a separate sum of this list, so exclusion is honored where it matters and visibility is preserved elsewhere. (Open question: whether the report's headline score reads from `getObjectivePerformance` or recomputes from `statisticsInitiativePerformance` — verify during impl.)

4. **Persist the field.** Add `statType` to the save/edit request DTO `InitiativeSaveRequest` (`backend/web/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/web/controller/initiative/request/InitiativeSaveRequest.java`, current fields lines 24-103) and map it through `InitiativeConverter.convertInitiativeSave`. Endpoints: `POST /api/initiative/save` and `POST /api/initiative/editInfo` (`backend/web/src/main/java/com/uae/gov/web/controller/initiative/InitiativeController.java:113-129`). Default to `0` when the client omits it.

### Frontend

1. **Form field.** Add a **required `Type` `Select`** to `frontend/src/pages/Initiatives/InitiativesMgmt/components/InitiativesAddAndEdit/InitiativesAddForm.tsx`, placed in the **General Info section directly under the date** field (not next to Budget). Label it **"Type"** to match the KPI form's Counted/Excluded field label. Mirror the KPI field: `field={'statType'}`, `rules={[{ required: true }]}`, `initialValue={strategyApiTypeEnum['Counted in Stats']}`, options reusing `strategyApiTypeEnum` and the `kpis:informationTypes.inStats` / `exStats` labels (or new initiative-namespaced keys with identical text).

2. **State / type.** Add `statType?: number` to `initiativeGeneralInfoState` in `frontend/src/store/initiative.ts:11-38`, and thread it through the save/edit payloads `initiativeInfoSave` / `initiativeInfoEdit` in `frontend/src/service/apis/initiative.ts`.

3. **List marker.** In the initiatives list, render a neutral/grey Arco `Tag` (no icon/emoji) on rows where `statType === 1`, with a tooltip explaining "Excluded from the cycle score; progress is still tracked." Mirror the tag conventions used elsewhere in the system.

4. **Locale.** Add EN + AR keys under the initiatives namespace (`frontend/src/locale/en/initiatives.json`, `frontend/src/locale/arEG/initiatives.json`); the option text can reuse the KPI strings to stay identical.

### Effort estimate

- Backend: ~0.5 day (column + migration + one aggregation filter + DTO field).
- Frontend: ~0.5 day (form field + list marker + locale + type plumbing).
- QA: ~0.5 day.
- **Total: ~1.5 dev-days.**

### Feasibility verdict (for the dev team)

Workable and low-risk. The initiative aggregation query (`CycleInfoServiceImpl:1693`) is **separate** from the list/detail/report queries, so a single flag cleanly removes an initiative from the score while leaving it visible everywhere else — exactly how the KPI flag already behaves. **A duplicate initiative is not required**; a single `statType` flag on the existing initiative, toggled when it carries over, fully satisfies "visible + reportable but not counted."

---

## Open questions / 待澄清

> Minor items only — the score-only scope is now locked (see below).

1. **List marker wording & style.** What label/tooltip text should the list marker use ("Not counted" / "Excluded from stats"), and should the report add a footnote noting excluded initiatives? Proposed default: a neutral grey tag "Excluded from stats" with a tooltip "Tracked, but not counted in the cycle score." / 列表标记文案与样式；建议用中性灰标签 + 提示。

2. **Who can set it / role visibility.** Available to all initiative editors, or admin-only? Proposed default: same edit permission as the rest of the initiative form (whoever can edit the initiative can set this field). / 谁能设 —— 建议跟编辑举措的权限一致。

3. **⚠ Budget rollup for excluded initiatives — PENDING CUSTOMER CONFIRMATION (Amir 2026-06-17).** Locked decision 8 currently keeps an excluded initiative's budget/expense **in** the dashboard totals (score-only exclusion). Amir flagged uncertainty: the dashboard budget *might* be using **last year's budget figure**, so confirm with the customer (a) whether an excluded initiative's budget should still be counted, and (b) which budget figure the dashboard actually sums (current vs prior cycle). Until confirmed, decision 8 (budget still counted) stands as the default. / 预算合计是否计入被排除举措、且仪表盘用的是当年还是去年预算，待客户确认；在确认前按决策 8（仍计入）执行。

---

## Locked decisions / 已锁定决策

> Only decisions directly implied by the confirmed requirement.

1. **A plain general-purpose field — single flag, NOT a carry-over toggle.** R-450 adds one `statType` field to the initiative (mirroring KPI). It is NOT tied to carry-over logic; the system does no carry-over detection and nothing automatic. A duplicate initiative is not required — the user just sets the flag on whichever initiative they want excluded. Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 就是一个普通字段（单开关），不绑定结转逻辑，系统不做任何自动判断；不需要复制举措。

2. **No carry-over auto-detection.** The old draft's "detect incomplete prior-cycle initiatives and prompt the admin" banner is OUT of scope. Users manually create and flag initiatives themselves. Reason: a system that has to interpret "is this a carry-over?" is fragile; a plain field the user controls is simpler and more flexible. Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 不做"自动检测上周期未完成举措并提示"那套，纯手动。

3. **All existing / historical initiatives default to Counted in Stats.** The migration backfills every current initiative to Counted, so no current data's scoring changes. Exclusion is only ever an explicit forward choice. Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 所有历史举措默认"计入统计"，不改变现状。

4. **Mechanism mirrors the KPI exclude-from-stats setting.** Wording and semantics follow the KPI "Counted in Stats / Excluded from Stats" precedent. / 机制照搬 KPI 的"计入/不计入统计"，文案语义一致。

4b. **Field label = "Type"**, the same label the KPI form uses for its Counted/Excluded field (consistency). Placed in the **General Info section, under the date**. The field is **mandatory** (defaults to Counted, cannot be cleared to empty). Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 字段叫 "Type"（跟 KPI 一致），放在基本信息区日期下面，必填，默认计入。

5. **Default is Counted in Stats.** A newly-created initiative counts toward the score by default; exclusion is an explicit opt-in. / 默认"计入统计"。

6. **Excluded initiatives stay visible, updatable, and reportable.** Exclusion affects only the final-score calculation; the initiative remains in lists, detail, and reports. / 排除只影响最终计分，举措照常显示、可更新。

7. **Excluded from denominator, not zeroed.** An excluded initiative is removed from both the weighted numerator and the weight base, so it does not dilute the score — matching the KPI exclusion behavior. / 不计入时从分子和分母一起去掉，不是清零。

8. **Exclusion is SCORE-ONLY.** "Excluded from Stats" removes the initiative only from performance / score calculations (objective & cycle rollup, dashboard performance rings, performance/monthly/sector reports). It is **still counted** in budget/expense totals and initiative counts, and stays visible in lists, detail, Ongoing Cycle, late-submission, and still receives reminder emails — because it is still a real initiative with real budget and work in progress. Matches the KPI precedent. Locked by Eugene 2026-06-15. / 排除只影响绩效得分；预算合计、举措数量照常计入，列表/详情/进行中/迟交/提醒邮件照常。

---

## Related PRDs / 相关 PRD

- [R-576 KPI List — Completion Rate Column Fix for Excluded Percentage KPIs](../V3.3/[R-576]-excluded-percentage-completion-rate-display.md) — the KPI exclude-from-stats precedent this PRD ports to initiatives (`KpiStatTypeEnum` / `strategyApiTypeEnum`).
- [R-579 Committee Add KPI Form Fields](./[R-579]-committee-add-kpi-form-fields.md) — same-version KPI form work using the Counted/Excluded stat-type field; the initiative field reuses its option wording.

2	
3	> **Ticket number provisional (R-585) — confirm with PM / Feishu before approval. / 编号为临时占位（R-585），审批前请与 PM / 飞书核实。**

6	|---|---|
7	| **Version** | V3.4 |
8	| **Source / 来源** | Customer requirement raised at 2026-06-10 V3.4 planning meeting / 2026-06-10 V3.4 规划会议上客户提出的需求 |
9	| **Priority** | P1 |

14	---
15	
16	## Overview / 概述
17	
18	> 简述：临时周期发起这个功能现在只能一个一个挑人，而且一个临时周期只能选一个上报期。客户希望：(A) 既能一个一个挑人，也能"一次选一整个部门"，把整个部门一次性纳入；(B) 一个临时周期能同时覆盖多个上报期。
19	
20	The Temporary Cycle Initiation feature (Settings → Cycle Initiation Settings → Temporary Cycle Initiation) lets an admin open an ad-hoc reporting cycle for a chosen set of people. Two limitations surfaced in the 2026-06-10 V3.4 planning meeting:
21	
22	1. The admin can only assign **individual users one at a time**. There is no way to target a whole department in a single action.
23	2. A single temporary cycle can only cover **one reporting period**. To open the same cycle across several periods, the admin must create separate records.
24	
25	The customer asked for two things: **(A)** keep per-user picking *and* add the ability to select an entire department at once; **(B)** allow selecting **multiple periods** within one temporary cycle.
26	
27	---
28	
29	## The problem / 问题
30	
31	> 简述：现在临时周期发起，要给一个部门 30 个人开周期，Admin 得在人员选择器里一个一个点 30 次，慢、容易漏人。而且一个临时周期只能选一个期（比如只能选 Q1），如果要同时覆盖 Q1 和 Q2，就得建两条记录、发两遍邮件、维护两份分配名单。客户的实际场景往往是"整个部门、跨多个期"，现在的工具不匹配。
32	
33	**Assignee selection is per-user only.** To open a temporary cycle for an entire department of, say, 30 people, the admin must click each of the 30 users individually in the assignee picker. This is slow, error-prone (easy to miss someone), and has to be redone manually whenever the department's membership is referenced again.
34	
35	**One period per cycle.** A temporary cycle today targets a single period (e.g. only Q1). If the admin needs the same cycle to cover both Q1 and Q2, they must create two separate records — duplicating the name, dates, and the entire assignee list, and triggering two separate assignment emails.
36	
37	---
38	
39	## Current behavior / 当前行为
40	
41	> 简述：人员选择器其实已经是一棵"部门 + 人"的组织树，部门节点是显示出来的，但保存时系统只认到人这一层——选了部门节点不会被正确展开成成员。期目前是单选（项目/倡议是单选下拉，KPI 是单选级联）。
42	
43	**Assignee selection.** The assignee field is a checkable organisation tree that renders departments as parent nodes and users as leaf nodes under them. The admin expands a department and ticks individual users. Although department nodes are visible in the tree, the system only persists down to the **user level** — selecting a department node does not currently translate into "all users in that department." Each saved assignee becomes one per-user row in the backend; there is no department-scoped assignment.
44	
45	**Period selection.** The period control is **single-select**, and its type depends on the cycle type:
46	- Project type → a single-select dropdown of fortnightly options.
47	- KPI type → a single-select cascader (Quarterly → Q1–Q4, or Monthly → Jan–Dec).
48	- Initiative type → a single-select dropdown of months.
49	
50	Even though the underlying storage and the data transfer objects already represent the period as a **list**, the form collects exactly one value and wraps it into a one-element list on save. So in practice one cycle = one period.
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Expected behavior / 期望行为
55	
56	### A. Department + per-user selection / 部门级 + 单人级选择
57	
58	> 简述：人员选择器要同时支持两种用法：(1) 勾一整个部门 = 把这个部门当前的所有成员一次性纳入；(2) 继续像现在一样单独勾人。两种可以混着用（勾一个部门 + 再单独加几个别的部门的人）。锁定项 1 已定：勾部门 = 按"保存当下"把该部门成员快照展开成一个个人，存的还是一个个人，不是活的部门链接——以后部门加人不会自动进这个已建好的周期。
59	
60	The assignee picker must support **both** modes in the same field:
61	
62	1. **Select a whole department** — ticking a department node includes **every member of that department** in one action.
63	2. **Select individual users** — exactly as today, tick individual people.
64	
65	The two modes can be **mixed** in a single cycle (e.g. tick Department A as a whole, then additionally tick three named people from Department B).
66	
67	**What "select a department" means (locked):** ticking a department **snapshot-expands** to that department's current members at save time — each becomes an individual assignee on the record. It is **not** a live link to the department. If the department later gains or loses members, an already-saved cycle is **not** retroactively changed. This keeps the assignee set stable and predictable for a cycle that has already been communicated by email, and matches how the data is stored (per-user rows). A ticked department node is therefore expanded to its member user IDs before save (no department node is ever sent as if it were a single user). / 勾部门 = 保存当下把该部门成员快照成一个个人；不是活链接；建好后部门成员变动不影响已存周期；保存前部门节点先展开成成员 userId。
68	
69	The view-detail modal (R-461) continues to show the resolved **display names** of all assignees (not raw IDs), whether they were picked individually or expanded from a department.
70	
71	### B. Multiple periods / 多周期
72	
73	> 简述：一个临时周期能同时选多个期。比如 KPI 类型可以一次勾 Q1 + Q2 + Q3；倡议类型可以一次勾 Jan + Feb。选出来的多个期都属于同一条记录，共用同一个名称、起止日期、同一份分配名单、发一次邮件。列表"Period"列要把多个期都显示出来。
74	
75	A single temporary cycle can target **multiple periods** at once:
76	- KPI type → select multiple quarters and/or months (e.g. Q1 + Q2 + Q3).
77	- Project type → select multiple fortnightly periods.
78	- Initiative type → select multiple months.
79	
80	All selected periods belong to the **same** record — one cycle name, one start/end date pair, one assignee list, and **one** assignment email per assignee (not one email per period). The list page's **Period** column displays all selected periods for the row (e.g. comma-separated), not just the first.
81	
82	| 变更前 / Before (single-select) | 变更后 / After (multi-select) |
83	|---|---|
84	| ![Period single-select](artifacts/screenshots/r585-period-before.png) | ![Period multi-select](artifacts/screenshots/r585-period-after.png) |
85	| Selecting one period fills the field; choosing another replaces it. / 只能选一个期，选第二个会替换第一个。 | Multiple periods selected as tags (checkboxes in the dropdown); all belong to one cycle. / 多个期以标签形式选中（下拉为复选框），同属一条周期。 |
86	
87	**Period vs. date range (open):** the cycle still has an overall Start Date / End Date. Whether each selected period needs its own deadline/reminder, or whether all periods share the cycle's single date window, is an open question — see Open questions. The default assumption for this draft is **one shared date window for all selected periods**. / 默认所有期共用周期的一个起止日期窗口；是否每个期单独设截止/提醒为待澄清项。
88	
89	### Out of scope / 范围外
90	
91	- A live/dynamic department link that auto-updates a cycle's assignees when department membership changes (this draft uses a save-time snapshot). / 活动态部门链接（本稿用快照）。
92	- Per-user notification preferences / unsubscribe. / 用户级通知偏好。
93	- Changing the email template or adding per-period emails (one email per assignee regardless of period count). / 改邮件模板或按期发多封邮件。
94	- Push / in-app notifications. / 推送或应用内通知。
95	
96	---
97	
98	## Acceptance criteria / 验收标准
99	
100	| # | Criterion / 标准 | Priority |
101	|---|---|---|
102	| 1 | The assignee picker supports selecting a **whole department** in one action; ticking a department includes all its current members as assignees | P0 |
103	| 2 | The assignee picker still supports selecting **individual users** one at a time, exactly as before | P0 |
104	| 3 | Department and individual selections can be **mixed** in a single cycle | P0 |
105	| 4 | Selecting a department **snapshot-expands** to its members at save time (stored as per-user assignees); later department membership changes do not alter an already-saved cycle | P0 |
106	| 5 | A single temporary cycle can target **multiple periods** at once (KPI: quarters/months; Project: fortnights; Initiative: months) | P0 |
107	| 6 | All selected periods belong to one record sharing one name, one date window, and one assignee list; **one** assignment email per assignee regardless of period count | P0 |
108	| 7 | The list page **Period** column shows all selected periods for a row, not just the first | P0 |
109	| 8 | Edit mode repopulates all previously-selected periods and all assignees (resolved display names, not IDs); View mode renders them all read-only | P0 |
110	| 9 | Selecting a department node expands to its member user IDs on save (never sends a department node as if it were a single user) | P0 |
111	| 10 | No regression in R-443 create/edit/delete or R-461 view-detail + assignment-email behavior | P0 |
112	| 11 | English and Arabic both render the picker, period control, list, and modal correctly | P1 |
113	
114	---
115	
116	## QA test scenario / QA 测试场景
117	
118	| Setup | Steps | Expected |
119	|---|---|---|
120	| Admin role. A department (e.g. "Strategy Dept") with at least 3 members exists; at least one user belongs to a different department. KPI-type temporary cycle available. | 1. Settings → Cycle Initiation Settings → Temporary Cycle Initiation → Add. 2. Enter name, year, type = KPI, dates. 3. In the assignee picker, tick the whole "Strategy Dept" node, then additionally tick one individual user from another department. 4. In Period, select Q1 + Q2 + Q3 (multiple). 5. Save. 6. Reopen the row in View mode. 7. Reopen the row in Edit mode. 8. Check the list Period column. 9. Switch UI to Arabic and reopen the modal. | Step 3: department expands to all its members + the one extra user. Step 4: multiple periods accepted. Step 5: saved as one record; each assignee gets exactly one email. Step 6 (View): all assignee display names + all 3 periods shown read-only, no Save button. Step 7 (Edit): all assignees and all 3 periods repopulated correctly. Step 8: Period column lists all 3 periods. Step 9: picker, periods, and names render correctly in Arabic. |
121	
122	---
123	
124	## Proposed technical solution
125	
126	> English-only section. PM does not need to review beyond this point.
127	
128	Key insight from code investigation: **the multi-period change is almost entirely frontend** — the backend already stores periods as a list. The department-select is mostly a **frontend expansion** of an existing tree the picker already renders, plus a small save-path fix. No new tables or DDL are required.
129	
130	### Reuse existing (no change needed)
131	
132	- **Backend period storage already supports a list.** `TemporaryCycleEntity.period` is a single `String` column persisting a JSON-serialized list via `JSON.toJSONString(input.getPeriod())` — `service/.../cycle/impl/TemporaryCycleServiceImpl.java:152` (save) and `:171` (edit). Both `TemporaryCycleSaveInput.period` (`bo/input/TemporaryCycleSaveInput.java:33`) and the outputs (`bo/output/TemporaryCycleIDetailOutput.java:33`, `TemporaryCycleIListOutput.java:32`) are already `List<String>`. Detail/list queries already `JSON.parseArray(entity.getPeriod(), String.class)` (`TemporaryCycleServiceImpl.java:95`, `:116`). **No backend change for multi-period.**
133	- **Assignees already stored as per-user rows.** `sk_temporary_cycle_user` (`dao/.../entity/cycleInfo/TemporaryCycleUserEntity.java`) holds one `user_id` row per assignee; `saveUserList()` batch-inserts them (`TemporaryCycleServiceImpl.java:244`). A department expanded client-side to member user IDs persists with **no backend change**.
134	- **Org tree already includes departments.** The picker's `treeData` comes from `getUserTreeRequest()` and `transformUserOrgTree()` already builds department nodes (`value: 'dept_<id>'`) with user leaves (`value: <userId>`) — `frontend/src/pages/SystemConfiguration/CycleInitiationSetting/components/TemporaryCycleInitiation/index.tsx:212-231`. Department nodes are already rendered; the gap is purely in how a ticked department node is converted to user IDs on save.
135	- **R-461 email + view-detail** unchanged; `sendAssignmentEmails()` (`TemporaryCycleServiceImpl.java:219`) fires once per assignee.
136	
137	### Frontend — new / changed work
138	
139	File: `frontend/src/pages/SystemConfiguration/CycleInitiationSetting/components/TemporaryCycleInitiation/index.tsx`
140	
141	1. **Period control → multi-select (section B).**
142	   - Project type: change the `Select` (`:548`) to `mode="multiple"`.
143	   - Initiative type: change the `Select` (`:567`) to `mode="multiple"`.
144	   - KPI type: the `Cascader` (`:559`) needs `multiple` (Arco Cascader supports `mode="multiple"`), or a redesign of the period model into a flat multi-select if mixed quarter+month selection is required (see Open questions).
145	   - `handleSaveAndEdit` (`:522`) currently wraps the single value: `period: data?.type == Kpi ? data?.period : [data?.period]` — change so the non-KPI branch passes the array directly (the control now yields an array) instead of `[data?.period]`.
146	   - Detail repopulation (`temporaryCycleDetail` onSuccess, `:486-492`) currently does `period: res?.type == Kpi ? res?.period : res?.period[0]` — change to keep the full list for all types.
147	   - `getPeriodLabel` (`:254`) and the list Period column (`:350-354`) must render **all** periods (join the list), not just `periods?.[1]` / first element.
148	
149	2. **Department expansion on save (section A).**
150	   - Today `handleSaveAndEdit` maps each selected tree value through `getSecondElement = str => str.split('_')[2]` (`:520`, `:525-528`). For a user composite key `user_<dept>_<userId>_<idx>` this yields the userId. For a **department node** `dept_<id>` it yields `undefined`.
151	   - New logic: when a selected node key starts with `dept_`, walk that department subtree in `orgTreeData` and collect all descendant **user** leaf `userId`s; when it is a `user_` key, extract the userId as today. De-duplicate the union (a user picked both individually and via their department counts once). Send the resulting flat `List<Long>` userIds as `userList` — backend unchanged.
152	   - This change enables department selection (A) and makes a ticked department node resolve to its members instead of an empty reference.
153	
154	3. **Translations** — any new helper text (e.g. multi-select placeholders) added to `t_translation` plus fallback JSON `src/locale/en/cycleInitiationSetting.json` + `src/locale/arEG/cycleInitiationSetting.json`.
155	
156	### Backend — verify only
157	
158	- Confirm `period` JSON list round-trips for >1 element on save/edit/detail/list (the code already does this; add/confirm a test with multiple periods).
159	- Confirm `saveUserList` and the edit-time user delta (`TemporaryCycleServiceImpl.java:185-196`) behave correctly when a department expands to many user IDs (it should — it is a plain list).
160	- No new endpoints, no new tables, no DDL.
161	
162	### Effort estimate
163	
164	- Frontend: ~1.5 days (multi-select period controls + period round-trip + display, department-expansion + dedupe on save).
165	- Backend: ~0.25 day (verification + a multi-period/multi-user test).
166	- QA: ~0.5 day.
167	- **Total: ~2.25 dev-days.**
168	
169	---
170	
171	## Open questions / 待澄清
172	
173	1. ~~**Department snapshot vs. live link**~~ — **Resolved (Amir 2026-06-17):** save-time **snapshot** (not a live link). See Locked decision 1.
174	2. ~~**Mixed department + individual**~~ — **Resolved:** yes, mixing allowed (whole department + extra individuals). See Locked decision 2.
175	3. ~~**Per-period deadline/reminder**~~ — **Resolved (Amir 2026-06-17):** **one shared date window + one email** per assignee for all selected periods (no per-period deadline/reminder). See Locked decision 4.
176	4. ~~**KPI cascader multi-select shape**~~ — **Resolved (Amir 2026-06-17):** **mixed granularity is allowed** (e.g. Q1 + Feb in one cycle). The KPI period control must support selecting across quarters and months together. See Locked decision 5.
177	
178	---
179	
180	## Locked decisions / 已锁定决策
181	
182	1. **Department selection = save-time snapshot, not a live link.** Ticking a department expands to its current members at save time; later membership changes do not propagate to an already-saved cycle. Locked (Amir 2026-06-17).
183	2. **Department-tick and per-user selection are mixable** in one cycle. Locked (Amir 2026-06-17).
184	3. **A single temporary cycle can target multiple periods at once.** Locked (Amir 2026-06-17).
185	4. **All selected periods share one date window and one assignment email per assignee** — no per-period deadline/reminder, one email regardless of period count. Locked (Amir 2026-06-17).
186	5. **Mixed period granularity is allowed** — the admin can combine quarters and months (e.g. Q1 + Feb) in one cycle; the KPI period control supports cross-granularity multi-select. Locked (Amir 2026-06-17).
187	
188	---
189	
190	## Related PRDs / 相关 PRD
191	
192	- **R-443** — original Temporary Cycle Initiation (Settings → Cycle Initiation Settings). Introduced the per-user picker and single-period model this PRD extends.
193	- [**R-461** Temporary Cycle Initiation — Email + View Detail](../V3.3/[R-461]-temporary-cycle-enhancements.md) — added the assignment email and read-only view modal; both are reused unchanged here.
